entry.title: Solace from Scratch: A Beginner’s Guide to Setting Up Your First Event Broker
entry.content:
I'm excited to share my very first blog with the SAP community! While working on real-time integration scenarios, I recently got hands-on with the Solace Event Broker, a powerful platform that supports event-driven communication using topics and queues.

In this blog, I’ll walk you through the complete end-to-end setup of Solace — from signing up on the Solace Cloud Console to configuring services, creating queues, and testing message flows using the built-in tools. Whether you're a beginner exploring event mesh technology or preparing to integrate Solace with enterprise systems, this guide is designed to help you get started with confidence.

Solace Event Broker, also known as Advanced Event Mesh (AEM), enables event-driven architecture by allowing seamless integration between enterprise systems like SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) and message brokers.

 

  1. Access the Solace Platform

To get started, visit https://solace.com and click on the Login button at the top right corner. From there, you can create a free trial account to access the Solace Cloud Console and begin setting up your messaging service.

 

2. Sign Up / Log In: If you’re new to Solace, click on Sign Up to register as a new user. If you already have an account, simply log in using your valid credentials and Jump to Step4.

 

3. Provide Valid Sign-Up Details

If you're signing up for the first time, ensure you enter all the required and accurate information during the registration process to successfully create your account.

 

4.  Access the Solace Console

After successfully logging in, you will be redirected to the Solace Cloud Console, where you can begin managing your messaging services.

 

5.  Navigate to Cluster Manager

On the left-hand panel of the Solace Console, under Component Services, click on Cluster Manager.
The Cluster Manager is where you create and manage your Solace messaging services (brokers). It allows you to define the environment, cloud provider, region, and service type to provision a messaging service tailored to your needs.

 

6. Create a New Service

To create a new Solace messaging service, click on Create Service and provide the following details:

  • Service Name

  • Environment (e.g., dev, test)

  • Cloud Provider (AWS, Azure, etc.)

  • Region (choose the one closest to your location)

For learning and development purposes, select the Service Type as Developer, which is a free-tier option suitable for testing and non-production use. It allows you to explore key features like topics, queues, and basic message flow without incurring any cost.

Once all the details are filled in, click on Create Service.

 

7.  Service Added to Cluster Manager

Once the service is successfully created, it will appear under the Cluster Manager dashboard.
Locate the newly created AWS Developer Service card (or the corresponding cloud provider you selected), and click on it to open the service details and launch the messaging console.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

9. Use Basic Authentication

To connect with your Solace Event Broker, either for testing within the console or integrating with SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)—you’ll need to authenticate using Basic Authentication.

From the “Try Me!” tab:

  • The Client Username is auto generated

  • The Client Password is masked but can be copied using the icon beside the field

  • These credentials allow you to publish and subscribe messages securely via the broker

These credentials are essential for establishing a secure connection from CPI to Solace using the AEM (Advanced Event Mesh) adapter, especially when sending data to queues or topics.

 

10. Data Publishing Methods

  1. Topics (Publish/Subscribe Pattern)
  • Allows broadcasting messages to multiple subscribers.
  • Every subscriber listening to a topic receives a copy of the message.

Delivery Modes:

  • Direct: Messages are sent immediately and not stored in the broker.
  • Persistent: Messages are stored until acknowledged by the receiver.

  1. Queues (Asynchronous Processing)
  • Used to store and manage messages between systems.
  • You need to provide a Queue Name to place data in the queue.

Delivery Modes:

  • Direct: Messages are sent immediately and not stored in the broker.
  • Persistent: Messages are stored until acknowledged by the receiver.

 

 

11. Subscribing to Data

Subscribing to data in Solace is a straightforward process once the connection is established. It uses the same authentication credentials as the publisher to ensure consistency and security.

  • Use the same client username and password that were used to publish messages.

  • After a successful connection, navigate to the Subscriber section in the Try Me! console.

  • Enter the exact topic path used during publishing. Solace uses hierarchical topic structures, so the path must match exactly for the subscriber to receive the intended messages.

 

12. Verify Data and Status

After subscribing, you can check the data and its status at the bottom of the Subscriber Panel

 

This guide walked you through the complete setup of Solace Event Broker, from account creation to message publishing and subscription.
With topics, queues, and authentication configured, you now have a solid foundation for event-driven messaging.
These basics are essential before moving on to real-time integration scenarios.
In the next part, we’ll connect Solace with SAP CPI using the AEM adapter for seamless message flow.

entry.title: Scheduling Background Jobs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud: A Practical Guide
entry.content:
Step 1: Create the ABAP Class with Required Interfaces

You’ll start by creating a class that contains the logic to be executed in the background.

Interfaces Implemented:

  • IF_OO_ADT_CLASSRUN: Allows testing the class directly in ADT.
  • IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT: Defines job metadata and input parameters.
  • IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT: Contains the actual logic to be executed in the background job.

Your class might look like this:

CLASS zcl_insert_sample_data DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES: if_oo_adt_classrun,
                if_apj_dt_exec_object,
                if_apj_rt_exec_object.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS: insert_sample_data.
ENDCLASS.
METHOD if_apj_rt_exec_object~execute.
* " This is the method that will be called by the background job
DATA: application_log           TYPE REF TO if_bali_log,
          application_log_free_text TYPE REF TO if_bali_free_text_setter.
   TRY.
   insert_sample_data( ).
application_log = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(
                               header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object = 'XYZ' " Use your custom log object/subobject
                                                                       subobject = 'INSERT' ) ).
        application_log_free_text = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create(
                                        severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_information " Or c_severity_success
                                        text     = 'Sample data inserted successfully.' ).

        application_log->add_item( item = application_log_free_text ).
        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log(
            log                        = application_log
            assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true  ).

      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_error).
        " Log the error message to the Application Log
*        application_log = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(
*                               header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object = 'ZSAMPLE_DATA'
*                                                                       subobject = 'INSERT' ) ).
*        application_log_free_text = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create(
*                                        severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
*                                        text     = |Error inserting data: { lx_error->get_text( ) }| ).
*
*        application_log->add_item( item = application_log_free_text ).
*        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log(
*            log                        = application_log
*            assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true  ).

        " Re-raise the exception to signal job failure to the framework
*        RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_apj_rt_exec_object
*          EXPORTING
*            previous = lx_error.

    ENDTRY.

This class inserts sample data into a custom table and logs the result using CL_BALI_LOG.

📘 Step 2: Create a Job Catalog Entry in ADT

Once your class is ready, the next step is to register it in the Job Catalog using ADT.

How to do it:

  1. In ADT, go to the Project Explorer.
  2. Right-click on your package → New → Other → ABAP Repository Object.
  3. Search for Job Catalog Entry.
  4. Provide:
    • Catalog ID: e.g., ZZ1_INSER_DATA
    • Description: e.g., Insert Sample Titles
    • Execution Class: ZCL_INSERT_SAMPLE_DATA
  5. Save and activate.

 

This step makes your class available for job templates.

🧾 Step 3: Create a Job Template in ADT

Now, create a Job Template based on the catalog entry.

Steps:

  1. In ADT, right-click your package → New → Other → ABAP Repository Object.
  2. Search for Job Template.
  3. Provide:
    • Template ID: ZZ1_JOB
    • Description: Insert Sample Titles
    • Job Catalog Entry: ZZ1_INSER_DATA
  4. If your class uses parameters, map them here.
  5. Save and activate.

 

This template will be used in the Fiori app to schedule the job.

🌐 Step 4: Create an Application Job Template in Fiori

Now switch to the Fiori Launchpad.

Steps:

  1. Open the "Manage Application Job Templates" app.
  2. Click Create.
  3. Fill in:
    • Template Name: e.g., Insert Sample Titles
    • Job Template ID: ZZ1_JOB (from ADT)
    • Job Catalog ID: ZZ1_INSER_DATA
  4. Save and activate.

This step makes the job template available for end users.

Step 5: Schedule the Job in Fiori

Finally, schedule the job using the Fiori app.

Steps:

  1. Open the "Schedule Application Jobs" app.
  2. Click Create.
  3. Select your Application Job Template.
  4. Set:
    • Start Time: Immediate or scheduled
    • Recurrence: One-time or recurring
  5. Click Schedule.

🧾 Application Logging

Your class uses CL_BALI_LOG to write messages to the application log. This is essential for monitoring and debugging.

Benefits:

  • Structured logging with severity levels.
  • Integration with job monitoring tools.
  • Easy to trace job execution history.

entry.title: Health Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM – Beginners Guide
entry.content:
Structure of this blog

This blog is structured to understand the following:

  • Health Monitoring Overview
  • Setup of health Monitoring
  • KPI Threshold Mapping
  • Creating Events in Health Monitoring
  • Alerts Handling in Health Monitoring
  • Conclusion

Health Monitoring Overview

Health Monitoring is a functionality in SAP Cloud ALM that provides an overview on the healthiness of all cloud services and on-premise systems connected to the Cloud ALM.

 We can check it from an application and customers perspective. The idea of Health Monitoring is to provide a central view of all our services in our landscape and to have a central alerting capability.

 

Application works by constantly connecting metrics about health status of cloud services of systems, metrics regarding to the usage, usage of resources and these metrics that are regularly collected are sent to SAP Cloud ALM into Health Monitoring Application which displays the data we can use to monitor, do analytics, and configure alerting using thresholds.

In the Health Monitoring app we can check the health of monitored cloud services and technical systems from an application and customer perspective.

The metrics are collected on a regular basis and can be used to calculate the overall health of a managed component.                           

In detail, the following features are available:

  • In the home page of the app, we can see the current status of all cloud services or technical systems which are connected to the Health Monitoring app, grouped by the different service types. An overall health is calculated based on the number of metrics in warning or critical status.

 

  • The metric details for each monitored service or system are displayed when we select it from the home page. Each component or metric is represented by a tile which summarizes the current value and rating. By clicking a tile, we will get more details on all contained metrics.

  • For each metric you can display the historical values of the metric. Here you can identify trends or investigate at which time a specific resource shortage has occurred. This will provide metrics for:
  • The ABAP system (for example, work process utilization, short dumps, heap memory, ICM and gateway resources).
  • The HANA database (for example, service status and memory utilization).
  • Hosts (such as CPU, memory and disk utilization).

Setup of health Monitoring

  • Connect service or system to SAP Cloud ALM instance in Landscape Management. Then we can set up the Health Monitoring itself.
  • The monitoring is activated automatically after we have subscribed to SAP Cloud ALM Service.

Add a system/service to be monitored.

 

Give the required details:

 

Configure the supported use case by switching the toggle ON.

 

KPI Threshold Mapping

We can either use the “Default SAP threshold” or can modify to a new threshold after customer landscape study to make the monitoring as optimized as possible.

 

Creating Events in Health Monitoring

We can also create events and map the below criteria for monitoring optimization.

 

  • Create Alert

Switch the toggle ON to enable Alerting

 

  • Send Email to
  • Switch the toggle ON to enable Alerting
  • Add the recipients by clicking on “+” sign,

 

 

  • It will populate the verified recipients, maintained in the Notification Management tile under Operations.

 

  • Send Operation Flow
  •  Switch the toggle ON to enable Operation Flow
  • Add the Operation flow by clicking on “+” sign,

  • It will populate the Operation Flows, maintained in the Operation Automation tile under Operations.

What is Operations Automation?

With Operations Automation, we can automate procedures related to the operation of the system landscape. These procedures are called Operation Flows in the context of SAP Cloud ALM for Operations.

These Operation Flows could be one of the following procedures:

  • Standard operational procedures related to the operations of your SAP Solution Landscape.
  • Problem resolution procedures triggered by alert and event processing.

Operations Automation in SAP Cloud ALM offers several benefits:

  • It enables faster resolution of detected problems by providing automated operation flows. This ensures that issues are addressed efficiently.
  • The operation flows generate an audit trail which allows for easy tracking and analysis.

                  Register Operation Flow:

                  We can register new operation flows of type:

 

To register a new operation flow definition, enter the following data:

  • Endpoint: The endpoint is already filled based on the selected automation engine type. If multiple endpoints exist for one automation engine type, you can enter any endpoints from the value help.
  • ID: Select the process and workflow, intelligent RPA automation, or automation pilot command that should be triggered from SAP Cloud ALM from the value help.
  • Use Case: Select the relevant use cases that are to be assigned to the operation flow.
  • Input Reference: This is an optional parameter, which is applicable only for the Automation Pilot commands. If not, all the required input parameters for the command are filled by SAP Cloud ALM. Enter an input reference in Automation Pilot that fills the missing input parameters. Select the input reference during command registration in SAP Cloud ALM.

Once we have decided on external Operations Automation engine, we need to setup an end point in the Landscape Management, before we can start an automated or manual execution of an operation flow.

To create an endpoint in the Landscape Management app, Click on “Add” under the selected Service:

 

Prerequisites for the Operation Flow setup in SAP Cloud ALM are:

  • We have subscribed to SAP Automation Pilot or SAP Build Process Automation or both.
  • The SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Build Process Automation tenant are listed in Landscape Management as cloud services.
  • An endpoint point is created in Landscape Management to SAP Automation Pilot and/or SAP Build Process Automation.

Automated execution of operation flows:

  1. A monitoring use case detects a problem and sends a monitoring event to Intelligent Event Processing.
  2. Intelligent Event Processing triggers the assigned operation flow in SAP Build Process Automation or SAP Automation Pilot.
  3. The operation flow executes the relevant problem resolution steps.
  4. The triggered operation flow and its status are visible in alert details (if alert was triggered).

Manual execution of operation flows:

  1. A monitoring use case detects a problem and sends a monitoring event to Intelligent Event Processing.
  2. Intelligent Event Processing creates an alert and sends an email notification.
  3. The alert processor analyses the alert in the alert inbox, searches for a suitable operation flow and starts it.
  4. The operation flow executes the relevant problem resolution steps.
  5. The triggered operation flow and its status are visible in alert details.
  6. Alert processor confirms alert.
  • Send Chat Notification
  • Switch the toggle ON to enable Chat Subscriptions
  • Add the Chat Subscriptions by clicking on “+” sign,

  • It will populate the Chat Subscriptions, maintained in the External API Management

tile under Administration.

Refer  : Integration with an external chat system: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/intelligent-event-processing/integration-chat-systems.html?anchorId=section

  • Create Ticket
  • Switch the toggle ON to enable Incident creation
  • Add the Ticket Subscriptions by clicking on “+” sign,

  • It will populate the Ticket Subscriptions maintained in the External API Management tile under Administration.

Refer : Integration with an external ticket system: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/intelligent-event-processing/integration-ticket-systems.html?anchorId=section

 

  • Store Event Payload for 24 Hours
  • Switch the toggle ON to enable Store Event Payload for 24 Hours

When this option is enabled, the event payload is stored in the Intelligent Event Processing data store for 24 hours. We can then access the data store and consume the event payloads using the Raw Data Outbound Logs API for Intelligent Event Processing. 

Alerts Handling in Health Monitoring

The alerts are raised once the threshold has been met, Please note that consecutive Yellow or Red statuses will not trigger additional notifications.

Note: As of now, Cloud ALM does not send a new Alert/Event if it is resolved (from Red/Yellow to Green). 

We can filter the alerts by below criteria in Alert Management app of Cloud ALM under Health Monitoring.

Conclusion:

Health Monitoring application offers us the following features:

  • We can monitor the availability of services, systems, and components.
  • We can monitor the trends in the resource usage. For example, how many users are logged on or resources used by the service.
  • We can see information about certificates with their expiration date.
  • It will provide us with system monitoring capability in SAP Cloud ALM showing us for example issues on a database level or information about CPU memory usage.
  • We can check the reported errors and configure alerting.

Thank you for reading the blog!

entry.title: How can I overwrite an existing SAP Build Process Automation production project with QA changes in t
entry.content: In our SAP Business Technology Platform subaccount, we maintain two Build Process Automation projects—MiApp_QA (quality environment) and MiApp_PROD (production environment)—both within the same subaccount. Whenever we complete testing in QA, we need to apply exactly the same artifacts and deployed logic to the production project. However, the manual .mtar export/import in the UI prevents us from overwriting or renaming MiApp_PROD, and workarounds like unpacking the .mtar to edit .mtar are impractical. We are therefore seeking a native method—whether via the SAP BTP CLI, a REST API endpoint, or another SAP-recommended workflow—that allows us to update or overwrite MiApp_PROD directly with the latest version of MiApp_QA without creating a new project or performing manual package edits. Any concrete examples of commands, flags, or endpoints that enable this “overwrite” within the same subaccount would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: "You and the SAC Gatekeeper" — A Beginner’s Guide to Using OAuth with SAP Analytics Cloud SCIM API
entry.content:
Introduction
Imagine you're trying to automate user provisioning in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using the SCIM API. You’ve got your data ready — names, emails, roles — but wait! You can’t just walk in and create users. SAC is a secure system with a tough gatekeeper, and before you do anything, you need to understand how OAuth works in this world.

Let’s walk through this with a story 📖 — because tech can be fun too!

🎭 Characters in Our Story
You – An admin or developer who wants to create users in SAC.

SAC Guard – The security gatekeeper of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Token Shop – A place (OAuth server) where you exchange your credentials for an access token.

Spoiler
Chapter 1: You Arrive at the Gate

You: “Hi! I want to go inside SAC and create some users using the SCIM API.”

SAC Guard: “Whoa, not so fast! Who are you? Do you have a permission slip (token)? We don’t let just anyone in!”

You: “Umm… no, but I have a Client ID and Secret. My boss gave them to me.”

SAC Guard: “That’s not enough. Go to the Token Shop and exchange that ID and Secret for a token.”

Spoiler
Chapter 2: Visiting the Token Shop 

The Token Shop is actually the OAuth server. It verifies that you are trusted, and hands you an access token in return.

Request Token

You make a POST request to the OAuth token endpoint (e.g.):  

POST https://<oauth-server>/oauth/token

 With the following form data:

grant_type=client_credentials
client_id=<your_client_id>
client_secret=<your_client_secret>

Token Shop: “Here you go! Your access token is valid for 1 hour. Use this to talk to SAC.”
 

Spoiler
Chapter 3: Back at the SAC Gate

You: “Hey Guard! I have my access token now. Look!” 

SAC Guard: “Perfect! Now you're allowed to call the SCIM API and create users. Carry on!”

Example SCIM API Call:

 

POST https://<sac-tenant>/api/scim/Users
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
"userName": "john.doe@example.com",
"name": {
"givenName": "John",
"familyName": "Doe"
},
"emails": [
{
"value": "john.doe@example.com",
"primary": true
}
]
}

 SAC accepts your request and the user is created!

Spoiler
Chapter 4: One Hour Later…

SAC Guard: “Sorry, your token has expired. Please get a new one from the Token Shop.”

Just like a movie ticket, your access token has a limited validity (usually 1 hour). Once it expires, you need to repeat the process and get a new one.

Spoiler
Chapter 5: Key Takeaways (The Real Lesson)
  • OAuth is a protocol that controls access.
  • You use a Client ID + Secret to ask the OAuth server (Token Shop) for an access token.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) will only accept API calls if you present a valid access token.
  • This entire mechanism protects SAC from unauthorized access and enforces security in automation.
  • For automation, consider scripting token generation and refreshing every hour (or using a refresh token if supported).

Conclusion


This story-based analogy helps demystify OAuth in the context of SAP Analytics Cloud user provisioning. Think of OAuth as a polite but firm doorman system that ensures only the right guests enter — and only if they’re on time.

So next time you see "401 Unauthorized" from the SAC SCIM API, don’t panic — just visit the Token Shop again.

 

Reference: Thought inspired from @Matthew_Shaw the automation using the SCIM API.

https://github.com/SAP-samples/analytics-cloud-scim-api-samples . Matthew user guide has a lot of detail in it and also troubleshooting steps, that might be helpful and worth a visit.

entry.title: PIR price condition update when contract is created via BAPI
entry.content:
Please suggest on how we can update PIR price and conditions without skipping conditions logic through standard approach or if we can have any other FM/ BAPI available to perform this action.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: PIR price condition update when contract is created v...
entry.content:
To update PIR price and conditions without bypassing the standard conditions logic, customer needs to use standard function modules or BAPIs that respect pricing logic and don't simply overwrite fields at table level.
Customer can try also with ME_UPDATE_INFORECORD. This function module updates PIRs and uses the standard logic including condition updates as done in ME12/ME11. Also they can use Use BAPI BAPI_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN but its support for detailed condition handling is limited.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Catalog does not show the relationship between SAC story and DSP Analytical Model
entry.content:
just to say that in one of our DSP and SAC tenants the lineage works perfectly.
we can open the SAC story in the DSP Catalog and see the lineage up to datasphere.

But this is not working in another DSP/SAC tenant.

entry.title: Re: SAP MRM: What is the retention period of the job logs...
entry.content: Hi there, the solution given applies to S4HANA public cloud as well. please confirm.

entry.title: Re: Document Currency is not changing at invoice level
entry.content: May be currency would have been changed in customer master.  Not sure whether you again checked the sales order document currency

entry.title: Getting Started with iFlows in SAP Integration Suite: Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
👋 Hello fellow tech explorers ! If you are new to SAP CPI and wondering how to start- you are  at the correct place! 

📖  Introduction

SAP BTP Integration Suite is a comprehensive suite of integration tools that includes Cloud Platform Integration (CPI), API management and more. In this blog, I will walk you through the step-by-step implementation of a simple Integration Flow that we implemented- a Weather Alert iFlow,  let's get started!

📌  Overview of the Business Scenario

The objective of the iFlow is to fetch weather data periodically from some public API and send an alert if the weather meets some predefined conditions like Temperature>35°C.  

✔️Prerequisites:  

1.An active SAP BTP trial account with Integration Suite subscription and corresponding roles assigned to it. 

Note- Refer to Get an Account on SAP BTP Trial for creating a SAP BTP trial account and refer to Set Up Integration Suite Trial for subscribing to Integration Suite service.

2. Create an account on any open source public API (here we are using OpenWeather API ) and get a free API key from the dashboard.

3. Ensure there is a Gmail account with OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code setup in SAP CPI.                                   

Note- Refer to SAP Integration Suite - IMAP and SMTP connection to Gmail with OAuth2 Authorization Code.

 

Now let's go through the steps to implement the iFlow in a SAP BTP trial account.

Step 1: Go to SAP BTP Trial  , click on Sign In, give required credentials and select Sign in.

                   

 

Step 2: Next "Go To Your Trial Account" have to be selected.

                       

Step 3: Next we need to select the Subaccount and click Instances and Subscriptions under Services.

                                                 

                                                 

                                                 

 

Step 4: Choose Integration Suite under Subscriptions.                                             

                                                    

 

Step 5: Select Design → Integrations and APIs on the Integration Suite screen.

                                                                                 

 

Step 6: Package Creation

Now we have to create a package which is nothing but a container that stores and manages integration artifacts.

So we will select on Create button on the top right side of the Design screen.

                                                  

  

The below screen will appear where Name, Short Description fields needs to be mandatorily filled and Technical Name field will be autopopulated. After that,  Save button needs to clicked.

                                                 

                                                          

 

Step 7:  Integration Flow creation

Now we will be creating the iFlow within this artifact, so we need to click Artifacts →Add →Integration Flow .  A dialog box will appear; we have to fill Name and Description fields for the iFlow here.

                                                

After the fields are populated,  we can now create the iFlow after clicking on the Add and Open in Editor button.

 

Step 8: Addition of Elements for the iFlow

⏲ a.  Timer Start Event- Every automation needs a trigger- so in this case we will first select the Timer Start which will basically decide when the iFlow will get triggered.  To make the iFlow easy, we have selected  On Deployment button which means every time the Deploy button is selected, the iFlow will get triggered.

🔃b. Request Reply - Next we will be using a Request Reply element which will basically request weather data through HTTP Adapter from  OpenWeather API (a free open source weather API).

🔌 c. HTTP Adapter and OpenWeather API HTTP Receiver - We use HTTP adapter and connect it to a receiver (renamed as OpenWeather API ) to fetch weather data. Under the Connection tab, we mention the API endpoint for the OpenWeather API in Address field, name of city with our unique API key from OpenWeather to access the data in the Query field and GET in the Method field as we are fetching the data from the API.

  

                                                    

✍️d. Groovy Script - Now that we have got the weather data, it's time to make sense of it. We will be using Groovy Script to do that. The Groovy Script will:                                                                                                                       

  • Read the JSON weather response from the API.
  • Extract the temperature   
  • Sets dynamic properties like temperature, isHot and condition for use in the rest of the flow.

Below is the code snippet that we used-

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
def Message processData(Message message) 
{
    //Body 
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String);
    def json = new JsonSlurper().parseText(body);
    def temp = json.main.temp;
    message.setProperty("temperature",temp);
    message.setProperty("isHot", temp > 35);
    message.setProperty("condition",(temp>35) ? "Hot" : "Normal");
    return message;
}

e.🔀Router- Now its time to make decisions- either alert would  be send or not based on the weather data fetched from the OpenWeather API; router element comes handy in such situations. 

If temperature from the weather API is above the set threshold , then  Send Alert branch is followed else the Default Route branch is followed,i.e. the iFlow gets ended.

 

f. 📃Content Modifier - Content Modifier is a powerful tool for enriching message data ,i.e., it helps to set or modify the message body, add headers etc. In this iFlow, we are using Content Modifier to prepare the weather alert message body with the inclusion of relevant static as well as dynamic values in the Body field and selecting Expression in Type field.

                                                        

 

 

g. 🔚End Event - The End Event after the content modifier in this iFlow properly finalizes the alert flow after the message body has been prepared in the content modifier. Once the alert message is sent, this End Event closes the flow.  Another End Event is connected to the default path of the router (which we selected after selecting the Default checkbox  for this branch).

 h. 📬Mail Adapter and Mail Receiver - The Mail Adapter and Mail Receiver work together to send email alerts in this iFlow. Under Connection tab, below details have to be filled:                                                                        

Address smtp.gmail.com:587 
Proxy TypeInternet
Protection STARTTLS Optional 
AuthenticationOAuth2 Authorization Code
Credential Name IS_GMAIL 

Note: IS_GMAIL is the name of the previously created OAuth 2.0 credential under security material.

We also need to define mail attributes like To,CC,BCC (Recipient/Recipients of the alert),  From (Sender of the alert) , Subject under Processing tab.

 

📧Mail Alert Example

On deployment, let's say the temperature of the city exceeds the threshold set, recipients will receive the below example email alert:

 

                                               

 

📸iFlow Snapshot

 

                                                          

 

📑Key Learnings 

  • Got a basic idea of different iFLow elements like Timer, Request Reply, Content Modifier etc.
  • Handle conditional logic using Groovy Script and Routers.
  • Format and send email content with Content Modifiers and Mail Adapter. 
  • Use CPI to automate external API calls.

If you have any queries or found this blog helpful, feel free to leave a comment here!

entry.title: SAP FSM Smartforms
entry.content:
Hello,

we have a checklist Instance that the technicien fill with pictures using attachmentPicker with multiple attachments possible,

A business rule is created to generate another checklist pr-filled usingthe multiple pictures taken and have to be in same object AttachmentPicker. 

When using the action FSM webhook, it is not possible t add the pictures in the same attachmentpicker beauce it should not be in the same payload, even using the action 'Create object of type ChecklistInstanceelement does not work.

Any one has an idea ?

 

thank

bests,

entry.title: Alation HANA DB Connector in violation of HANA Runtime License
entry.content:
Alation is a market leader in the Data Catalog space.  They show up in any kind of web research with BW, ECC, and other SAP connectors.  All of those connectors are really the same, an ODBC Connection to the HANA Database.  

As DBA's we know that metadata scans at the HANA level will bear no real fruit.  We should also know, that the Runtime License for S/4HANA does not allow ODBC connections by third parties.  In a nutshell, there appears to be no real support for S/4HANA to be connected to Alation.

If your company is assessing Alation, be very specific in your challenges on whether they work with SAP.  

That said, does anyone know if the Data Catalog in Datasphere will be a connector of any type to Third Party solutions like Collibra, or Alation?

The additional barcode functions are available in the formula editor under “Additional Functions.”

 

Thank you.
Best regards,

entry.title: University Alliances e Ânima Educação - Parceria Dual de Talentos
entry.content:
No último dia 16, demos início a um projeto inspirador ao lado do Grupo Ânima e do ecossistema SAP, com um propósito claro: apoiar a formação de futuros profissionais e aproximá-los do mercado de trabalho. 

Ao longo de duas fases, os estudantes aprenderão sobre as soluções SAP e como elas apoiam empresas em um mercado em constante transformação. 

A jornada inclui cursos oficiais, atividades práticas com o suporte direto dos professores da Ânima e da rede de parceiros SAP — incluindo Atos, Cast group, delaware Brasil, ITS Group, NTT DATA, NUMEN, SEIDOR e SPRO IT Solutions. Essa combinação entre teoria e prática cria um ambiente de aprendizado colaborativo, inovador e alinhado com as demandas reais do mercado.

Outro destaque do projeto é a conexão direta com empresas do ecossistema SAP. Os estudantes poderão colocar em prática os conhecimentos adquiridos e, ao longo da iniciativa, terão a chance de se certificar em soluções SAP, aumentando significativamente suas oportunidades de inserção profissional.

Com a expertise global da SAP, a excelência acadêmica do Grupo Ânima e a força do nosso ecossistema de parceiros, temos confiança de que estamos preparando uma nova geração de talentos, prontos para enfrentar os desafios do futuro com conhecimento, inovação e propósito.

entry.title: how to take backup of jobs spools in s4hana cloud?

If we would like to retain them for longer, is it possible to change retention period?

If not, is there anyway by which backup can be taken?

Also, please let us know if is it possible to send them over email?

Thanks

Regards,

Guido

entry.title: Re: Creating architecture diagrams with code
entry.content: @Florian_Kube yeah... I should probably revisit this hehe

entry.title: A Journey of Learning and Sharing – My Experience Teaching SAP at Universidade Portucalense
entry.content:
As this academic year closes in Portugal, I reflect on a significant chapter I had the privilege to be part of.

For many years, SAP and ERP systems have been central to my professional journey — complex, fascinating platforms supporting the heartbeat of countless organizations worldwide. This year, I took a step beyond my daily projects and brought this passion into the classroom.

At Universidade Portucalense in Porto, I was honored to teach the course Sistemas Integrados, part of the Information Systems for Management curriculum. Twenty students embarked with me on a semester-long exploration into the world of integrated enterprise systems.

What made this experience so enriching was not only the content, but also witnessing young students taking their very first steps into the SAP ecosystem. Through hands-on exercises on a live SAP S/4HANA system, they navigated business processes, created master data, posted financial transactions, and explored real-time analytics, both through the traditional SAP GUI and the modern Fiori interfaces.

I could see curiosity turning into understanding, and theoretical concepts transforming into practical skills. It’s always inspiring to watch that moment when the pieces start to come together — when the student realizes how companies are interconnected through these systems, and how technology drives daily business decisions.

But this journey also reminded me of something deeper: bridging the gap between academia and the business world is essential. Giving students early exposure to the tools and challenges that companies face daily is one of the best ways to prepare them for tomorrow’s job market demands.

Of course, none of this would have been possible without the support of Makevalue, which allowed me to teach these classes during my professional schedule. I’m deeply grateful for this opportunity to share my knowledge and, in return, to be energized by the enthusiasm and fresh perspectives that only students can bring.

Finally, a heartfelt thank you to Universidade Portucalense—an institution that values academic rigor and fosters strong connections with the business community, providing students with practical experiences that will serve them well throughout their careers.

I was a student at Universidade Portucalense, both for my undergraduate and master's degrees.

Teaching is always a privilege. And when it aligns with what drives you professionally, it becomes a truly special experience.

#SAPCommunity

entry.title: IBP - Cannot assign product in Lifecycle Management after deleting Manual Forecasting
entry.content:
Hello,
I previously created a Manual Forecast record for a product in SAP IBP to test the forecasting functionality. After testing, I deleted the Manual Forecast assignment and also removed the associated planning object.

However, when I try to assign the same product in the Manage Product Lifecycle application, I receive the following error:
“Product xxx is used in manual forecasting. References cannot be saved.”

It seems the system still considers the product as being used in Manual Forecasting, even though all related data has been deleted.

Could you please advise how I can fully release this product so it can be used in the Product Assignment function of the Lifecycle Management application?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Do you use the "Group Members" assignment in a Group...
entry.content: You likely don't have the Manage Groups authorization.

entry.title: SAP Fiori ALP - Visual Filter does not work when many records
entry.content:
Hi Community!

I am having a problem with Fiori Analytical List Page (ALP).

I created a project to display some charts based on the data we have (Dumps), it starts working properly with a small amount of data, however when the data is more realistic it starts failing. For example: Less than 200 records. Everything is working fine!



More than 200 records: Visual Filters does not load any more and above 700 records approx the main chart does not load anymore.



I am learning about this technology, so I am not sure what the problem is

I was thinking in maybe limit these charts to the top N values

Visual Filter: Top 3-5 or Last 5 records.
Bar Chart: Top 20 records

But not sure if that will help me to solve it and also I don't know if is possible.

Thanks!

We need to integrate Qualtrics with Emarsys to sync contacts between both platforms, and we’re considering acquiring SAP Integration Suite.

Could you help me understand what happens “inside” the platform — how it connects both systems and handles the integration?
Also, would you happen to know who I can contact to get pricing information for this product?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Dump issue with MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
We have created RAP class method which will be called during Sales order creation using entity I_SalesOrderTP.
Method is declared FOR ENTITY EVENT salesorder~created and sales order line item field rejection reason is updated using MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp
IN LOCAL MODE ENTITY salesorderitem
UPDATE FROM lt_update ( sales line item filled with rejection reason field)
FAILED ls_failed

The above modify statement causes dump 'The ASSERT condition was violated'. Could you please let us know if MODIFY ENTITIES can be used for 'ENTITY EVENT salesorder~created' Please provide solution to update sales order line item field with values during sales order creation.

Thanks & Regards,
Rajeswari

Latitude and Longitude are created as attributes of the geographic/location dimension. Its not the other way round.

entry.title: Custom recording dictionary adding extra blank space
entry.content:
This is something I've been experiencing for a while now.

I've created a custom recording dictionary which I use for almost all SEN projects. For whatever reason, I always get an extra blank space after calling the value for the input text. My dictionary entries are all clean and don't have any extra white space anywhere.

Looking at the HTML for this, it seems to add this space on its own right before the closing SPAN tag (<span style="font-weight: bold;"> $#{text_d} </span>.</p>) .

Any suggestions as to how I can fix this?

entry.title: Need help with figuring out technical name for "As of Date"
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

We are trying to create a new report in SQ01. Customer has requested to add "As of Date" field as a selection category. We tried to find the technical name but failed. Can someone please let us know under which Table name and Technical name for "As of Date" comes. 

We tried with F1 - technical information but still no correct answers. It shows ZFBDT as technical name in table BSEG but when checked in SE16N it shows the Baseline date as ZFBDT. What are we missing here. 

Product Version: EHP8 - SAP ECC 6.0

entry.title: Re: Cannot convert data type from CHAR20 to INT8 in CDS v...
entry.content:
BufferDays shows 4 different values: 0,1,2 and initial. The initial value needs to be treated separately, it cannot be casted to INT8. Therefore, try the following statement:

case when _YY1_SCM_CBO_MABD_WINDOW.BufferDays is initial then 0
  else cast( _YY1_SCM_CBO_MABD_WINDOW.BufferDays as ABAP.INT8 )
end

entry.title: Re: How to transfer BRF+ value in TCCS
entry.content: I have same issue. I created a condition TCM_AMNTFR with BRF+ Expression. But there is only data access definition inside the condtion. there is no chance to maintain the expression. Totally confused...Note given above is not explaining the concept . Can any one help to explain how BRF+ gets assigned to condition type/condition?

entry.title: Issue with Fiscal Week Configuration in SAC Planning Model
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm working on creating a Date dimension in a planning model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

I've successfully configured the fiscal year to start in May and to be labeled by the year in which it ends.

However, I need the fiscal week to start from the first Thursday of each month. Currently, the dropdown only allows selecting Saturday, Sunday, or Monday as the week start day.

Could anyone advise on how to achieve this or suggest a workaround?

Thanks in advance!

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Integrated Business Planning for demand RISE with SAP

entry.title: Re: Issue with Data Types: Batch Load Data into a Custom...
entry.content:
in such cases , you need to check your WSDl file and try to see the complete format over there for that particular fields like- 

<Property Name="BufferDays" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="20" sap:label="Buffer Days"

Therefore you need to convert/format your field in excel like wise and you are good to go.

entry.title: Re: Enabling Multiline Editing Capabalities in RAP
entry.content:
Hello,
Did you work with odata v2 or v4 as on data v2 there is no create button at the composition level ??

entry.title: Re: Recap Series: SAP Community members speaking at SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
@moshenaveh for SIT session recaps I usually base the blog on the YouTube recording. In those cases AI helps me put together something more technical about documenting the session and linking to the full video, not adding much of a personal voice.

But for personal blogs, your tip about training the model with old posts works. I’ve done that before. What I like most is recording my thoughts in MS Word using voice. I use some blog key points to guide the AI and always ask it to keep my tone. That’s what helps make the blog feel more like me. Appreciate the ideas, thanks!

entry.title: Re: DEBUG Custom logic S4HANA CLOUD
entry.content:
You may write below syntax within your custom logic code :

cl_ble_trace_writer=>write_info_message( 'Pass8' ).

Here instead of PASS8 - you may add your internal variables as well. Above syntax would ensure you if the code is triggered and you may see this variable in CUSTOM LOGIC TRACING app.

entry.title: Re: Tax procedure in S4HANA public cloud
entry.content: Were you able to get answer from this? I am also looking for the same. Thanks!

entry.title: Using the BPML in a Cloud ALM Project Set up for transport management
entry.content:
We are a government that has just started on our SAP Cloud ALM journey. The first phase of our project is to replace STMS and use Cloud ALM to do all our transports to our S/4HANA Private Cloud environment. I am after some advice on how to set up Projects within Cloud ALM using the BPML either as separate projects, the use of workstreams or tags.

I want know if other SAP customers have used the BPML structure to set up Cloud ALM Projects and if they have come across any issues during the set up or any issues after they have set it up so that we don't fall into the same trap.

I'm happy to arrange a meeting to discuss or use the blog for some advice.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Loading/Creating Story Reports in Non-Prod...
entry.content: Hola, me está sucediendo lo mismo. Intenté todo lo que dicen, inclusive reiniciar el router de inetrnet, conectarme desde otra red, a través de otro navegador y nada. Por favor, si pueden brindarme otra solución, lo agradecería. GRACIAS!!!

entry.title: PowerDesigner 16.6 SP06 X64 dependencies on Microsoft Visual C++ 2010
entry.content:
Hello,

Is Power Designer 16.6 SP06 X64 16.6.6.5645 dependencies there on  Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 ?
Is it is advised to update to latest version of Microsoft VC++ Redistributable for win 11?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: PowerDesigner 16.6 SP06 X64 dependencies on Microsoft...
entry.content:
Hello,

PD 16.6 and PD 16.7 versions prior to 16.7 SP08 require specific frameworks, including the mandatory Microsoft Visual C++ 2010, which cannot be replaced or removed.
However, PD 16.7 SP08 requires the Microsoft Visual C++ 2022 runtime libraries.
This information is also available in KBA 3483140 - Which Visual C++ runtime libraries does PowerDesigner require?

Please note that PD 16.6 has reached its end of life in November 2021.

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Need help with figuring out technical name for "As of...
entry.content:
(I suppose you are working on FI item reports)

As far as I know, there is no field of this type in the FI database. The user can refer to the parameter present in the initial screens of the finance detail reports (FBLxN, FAGLL0x).

  • The item is cleared if it is cleared nowadays and the reference date is at least equal to the clearing date?
  • If not, the user should be contacted again to clarify their requirements.

entry.title: Re: Recap Series: SAP Community members speaking at SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
@Marssel700 Very cool idea to record on MS word!

Happy to support presenters to combine between the personal insights and AI tools when posting about their sessions. 

You can also ask it to embed SEO keywords according to target audience, which is very interesting as well.

entry.title: Re: Develop Powerful Analytical Queries using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
I am deep diving and testing the behavior of queries defined with "transient view entity" statement.

A thing that I notice is that when I consume attributes from am associated #DIMENSION, the execution behaviour for me is not good:

1) Application server (AS) reads from the #CUBE view with aggregation (group by).

2) Application server issues a FDA Write to the database putting every attribute ID to join with master data of the #DIMENSION view.

3) the join with fact is effectively done in AS !!!

I try to apply this annotation @analytics.settings.olapPushdown: #MAX

and I notice the correct change in RSRT in query properties.

But with trace ST05 I keep getting the "FDA Write" behaviour.

How can I control this? is this a limit of transient provider?

Thanks,
Alberto

entry.content: We are facing same error, did you find any solution ?

entry.title: Run as installation issue for SAPGUI / registry management
entry.content:
Hello

As I'm not the administrator of my workstation, I install the SAPGUI package I created with NwSapSetupAdmin via the "Run as" command, but the registries entries I've created only apply to the account that has administrative rights. How can I make the registry keys I've defined apply to the end user?

Thank you for your help

Eric

entry.title: Print Setup - Show PDF - Grey and White - not colored
entry.content:
Hello all,

It is requested pdf of completed forms that users download to be colored, as it appears in the "print setup" pop-up page.

Is this possible?

Thank you in advance,

Vasso

entry.title: Re: Migración de datos a SAP S/4HANA 1909
entry.content: En el caso de la migracion de la cuentas que Gestionan partidas abiertas Bancarias como se migran?

entry.title: Re: BTP Workzone (standard) update config to allow additional origin
entry.content: Hi I'm currently on a client and we have the exact same issue. Were you able to find a solution to this?

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça
entry.content:
Your post is truly inspiring, @Marssel700 @! It's amazing to see how you've found a perfect balance between your SAP career and personal life.

Porto de Galinhas seems like incredible destination - I hope to visit it in an upcoming SIT event in Brazil 🙂

Thanks for sharing it with the  community.

entry.title: Re: Error while deploying SAPUI5 app via visual studio co...
entry.content: Even after adding deployment configuration from application wizard via which we create SAPUI5 app file of ui5-deploy.yaml was not added to my project. So via Fiori:Add Deployment Configuration generator i have added the deployment configuration then ui5-deploy.yaml file was available in my sapui5 app in visual studio then by using both build and deploy command i was able to upload the app to onpremise server.

entry.title: S/4 Hana conversion to margin analysis
entry.content:
Hi:

We upgraded from ECC 6.0 to S/4 Hana 2022 (on Rise) in August of 2023.  Our upgrade was just a technical upgrade, no new functionality.  We had/have costing based COPA active and want to look at turning on margin analysis now.  I am looking for any information from those who have made this conversion already.  We are trying to determine level of effort and what it will take to complete all the necessary evaluations. In my searching - I found documentation for those doing this as part of your S/4 migration but am not finding much on doing it stand alone after migration.

I would appreciate any guidance.

entry.title: Re: ABAP: Installation Guide
entry.content: All these ones: https://www.google.com/search?q=blog+post+ABAP+Trial+site%3Asap.com

entry.title: Delivery manual attachment is saving as .txt instead of .pdf (archived document are okay) in WebGui
entry.content:
Hello,

My delivery has two attachment, one created by an output in archive mode ("Bon de livraison"). Other one, i added myself with VL02N, it's a classic PDF file ("croix").

When with webgui I try to display them, both looks like same.

But then I try to save them, "Bon de livraison" let me save in pdf format whereas "croix" save a txt file.

I found this note: https://me.sap.com/notes/2404718 but i dont want to prevent display pdf in browser given that it works for "Bon de livraison".

If you have any idea why behaviour differs.

Thanks, and best regards.

entry.title: Re: 🇺🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Houston, TX, USA)
entry.content: We will supply all backend system access. You just need to bring a laptop to work from.

entry.title: Re: An Update to the HCM Payroll Bridge options for SAP SuccessFactors clients: Part 3
entry.content: Can be migrated to ECP, sone user exits do not exist and modifications could not be migrted.

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana conversion to margin analysis
entry.content:
Hi @kdudgmo1023 

You can refer to the below blog for step by step guide from migration from costing based COPA to Account based COPA. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-members/steps-by-step-guide-migration-from-costing-based-copa-to-account-based-copa/ba-p/14090125

Also I have one question, as you mentioned you are already migrated to S4 HANA, how come you have not activated Account based COPA as its mandatory in S4 HANA. 

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Dunning in Fiori
entry.content:
@Bohdan 
Hi Bohdan,
Thank you for sharing the article, according to your article, I did realize, by the way, have you tested using BRF + with Email Template to send emails? When I tested it, the PDF can be generated normally, but when I press F3, it doesn't trigger the event response.😁

entry.title: 2025 WBS Settlement to Assets is failing for Down payments
entry.content:
Hi Experts.

We are experiencing an issue during the settlements from Wbs to Assets.

Our Projects have a structure that allows the Asset be object receiver only in settlement rule for 2nd level wbs element. So whatever is posted in wbs from level 3 to higher is rolled up to level 2. Anyway the Down Payment assigned to level 3,4 or 5 and then rolled up to level 2 cannot be settled to Assets.

Questions:

Is this a SAP Standard depending on GL automating determination during settlements? Is that a SAP Standard Solution to this scenario in SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA?  

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: 🇺🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Houston, TX, USA)
entry.content: Thank you for confirming Thomas.

entry.title: How Loading date is calculated in sale order
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m trying to understand how the Loading Date is calculated in SAP during sales order processing. And the field is available in VBEP table.

Could someone please explain

Best regards,

Bharath

entry.title: Re: How do I apply the "Enabled Single-Sender" Email Addr...
entry.content: Thank you Jordi! I've gone ahead and voted/commented in the enhancement request!

entry.title: Manager (DACS) cannot manage the global assignment of his employee (DAZO)
entry.content:
Note: he can "ADD" a global assignment. But once added, he cannot manage it. This is strange.
What would happen if we also grant edit possibilities to the (local) HR Administrator role?

FYI:

1. Without adding in a target group, manager has to manage his employee's global assignment. Is there any way that we can add it from permissions....!

2. Also Is there a way we can create a dynamic group based upon the field "Is On Global Assignment"?

entry.title: SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.240) SP 2402 (64-bit) ErrorLicence Control Center Acces The License
entry.content:
Buenos días con todos, tengo el siguiente problema con mi servidor:
1)Se actualizo el windows server 2022 Standar y con sql 2019 standar con las ultimas actualizaciones de windows, resulta que luego le di una reinicia, anteriormente todo me cargaba muy bien luego que se actualizo y lo reinicie al momento de ingresar a SAP con mis credenciales manager ya no pude intregarme sale el siguiente mensaje "Unable to get access token. Contact Administrator." 

2) Intente ingresar al Service manager para ingresar mis servicios mediante el navegador chome u otros con mi usuario B1SiteUser, pero me sale el mensaje de: 

No se puede conectar a la URL de descubrimiento del servicio de autenticación
https://Serversap24:40020/auth/realms/sapb1/.well-known/openid-configuration . Para
más información, consulte la nota SAP 3237004.
Es muy probable que la dirección del servidor de autenticación no sea accesible o que haya un problema con el certificado.
¿Desea consultar esta página en una nueva versión de Windows?

3) Desinstale las últimas actualizaciones de Windows pero el problema persiste.

4) ingrese a los servicios de windows y reinicie los servicios de SAP pero el problema persiste

 

Si cuentan con la solución de este problema, les agradeceremos dejarlo por este medio o adjuntarme alguna documentación.

entry.title: Re: SAP Workzone: Task Center: Server returned HTTP respo...
entry.content: Hi, where would you go an "Uncheck option System Certificate for Logon"

entry.title: Re: This is regarding Poland e-invoicing, Is there any fi...
entry.content:
Greetings,

Pls check note - 3346323 - eDocument Poland B2B(KSeF) Troubleshooting Guide

Table EDOPLINV stores the KSeF related fields(Session Valid Upto, KSeF Reference No, Aquisition Timestamp etc.)

entry.title: Ariba : VIM Invoice attachment is not showing in /n/opt/vim_va2 tcode
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a issue related to Invoice.Where the Invoice attachemnt is not seen in the VIM analytic report

Steps:

1) Invoice is created for a Indirect PO from SBN

2) It is transferred through IDOC to SAP VIM Document type (InvoiceExportDirect)

When I check the VIM-tcode (/n/opt/vim_va2) the attachment is not showing Invoice(screenshot) for Indirect PO. However, for the direct PO it is getting displayed

Any idea what could be the reason?

IDOC:

 

VIM Analytics: Indirect PO [ Issue record]

 

 

 

VIM Analytics: Direct PO

entry.title: Document xxxxxx does not exist - Intercompany sales - RD04
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently implementing the Intercompany Sales process. Most of the flow is working as expected, but we’re encountering an issue during the creation of the AP invoice from message type RD04.

Although the IDoc is being generated, it fails with the error: "Document xxxxxx does not exist." The value "xxxxxx" is being pulled from the PO Number field in the sales order. However, in our process, no purchase order is being created in MM.

Here’s a brief overview of the steps we’re following:

  1. Sales order

  2. Delivery

  3. End customer invoice

  4. Intercompany invoice – RD04 – IDoc triggered to post vendor invoice

Since there is no MM purchase order in this flow, we’re unsure what the RD04-generated IDoc is supposed to match against on the MM side.

We would appreciate any insights or experiences from others who have implemented Intercompany Sales (not just standard Intercompany flow) and have successfully posted the vendor invoice without a corresponding PO.

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: Configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage Decision artifacts
entry.content:
@spatil_cengage no I don't see the app yet in the HTML5 repository

I already have a service instance configured in my CF space for SAP Build Process Automation with a service key

my BTP destination sap_process_automation_service_user_access is referring to this key with the html5-apps-repo / app_host_id

if I create a new service key for my SPA service instance, I will have a new html5-apps-repo / app_host_id, but just 1, there is always 1 app_host_id in the service key

are you saying that I should this new service key instead of my previous one and update my BTP destination with the new app_host_id?

entry.title: Re: How to extend fields for PO Mass upload template ?
entry.content:
Hello @Li_Grace 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, if you’re using the Migrate Your Data app or an RPA bot to upload Purchase Orders in bulk, adding new fields like Storage Location to the Excel template isn’t straightforward. You can only use the fields that SAP has already made available in the template.

Follow the steps below,

Check if the Field is Supported

  • First, verify whether Storage Location (LGORT) is part of the standard migration template for Purchase Order Items.
  • Use the app, Migrate Your Data – Migration Cockpit
  • In the template, open the sheet Field List → search under PurchaseOrderItem to see if Storage Location is available.

 If it's listed there,
You can add it to the Excel sheet (PurchaseOrderItem tab) and fill it in — the system will process it during upload.

 If it's not listed,
The field is not supported in the standard migration object and cannot be added manually to the template. You’ll need a workaround (see below).

 Workaround Options

1st option, Post-Processing Update via API or Mass Change App

  • Upload POs with available fields
  • Use Mass Changes to Purchase Orders app or API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to update the storage location afterwards.

2nd option, BTP RPA/Script Extension

If you're using SAP RPA

  • Extend your bot to update the storage location after creation using the Change Purchase Order app or API.

SAP officially recommends using

  • Mass Changes to Purchase Orders” Fiori app or
  • API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV
    ...to update or maintain fields like Storage Location if they’re not part of the initial upload.

 SAP References:

  • SAP Help: Migrate Your Data - Migration Cockpit and Mass Changes to Purchase Orders
  • Check also the migration object documentation in the Fiori app for the list of supported fields.

Hope it helps!

With kind regards

Chuma
